_schema_info.description: Word entry structure for the DISKNORY-FOR-AI brain. Each word is stored as a flat JSON object with 45+ fields covering identity, pronunciation, grammar, meaning, usage, AI intelligence flags, learning data, and system control. This schema is the single source of truth for all dictionary and AI-brain operations.

sections.A: CORE IDENTITY — word_id, word, lowercase, letters, letter_count, syllables
sections.C: GRAMMAR — part_of_speech, grammar_role, root_word, word_family, plural_form, tense_forms, comparative, superlative
sections.D: MEANINGS — hindi_meaning, english_meaning, definition_simple, definition_detailed, synonyms, antonyms
sections.E: USAGE EXAMPLES — example_sentence_en, example_sentence_hi, usage_context, common_phrases
sections.F: AI INTELLIGENCE FLAGS — 17 boolean flags for fast O(1) classification
sections.G: AI LEARNING DATA — confidence_score, learned_from, learned_date, times_used, last_used, user_corrections, alternative_meanings, related_concepts
sections.H: SYSTEM CONTROL — created_by, created_timestamp, modified_by, modified_timestamp, version, is_locked

validation_rules.word_id: MUST be unique. Format: LANG_NNN_wordname (e.g. ENG_001_hello)
validation_rules.word_lowercase_match: word field MUST equal lowercase of original word
validation_rules.confidence_score_range: confidence_score MUST be between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive
validation_rules.boolean_flags: All is_* fields MUST be true or false — never null
validation_rules.timestamp_format: All timestamp fields MUST use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)
validation_rules.version_increment: version MUST increment by 1 on every edit
validation_rules.null_allowed: null values are allowed for non-applicable fields (use null, not empty string)
validation_rules.is_locked: is_locked=true means AI cannot edit the entry without explicit user/admin permission

steps: Move entry to archive file (not permanent delete)
Log deletion in journal with timestamp
Add new meaning to alternative_meanings array
Recalculate confidence_score based on frequency of use
Add any newly discovered concepts to related_concepts

rules: May add new optional fields (schema.extensible = true allows this)
Must maintain backward compatibility with schema version 1.0.0
New required fields require schema version bump (e.g. 1.1.0)

future_expansion.hindi_words: Set language='hindi', keep identical structure, fill hindi_meaning with primary meaning
future_expansion.hinglish_words: Set language='hinglish', fill both hindi_meaning and english_meaning
future_expansion.new_languages: Copy schema, change language field, add translation fields as needed
future_expansion.new_field_types: Add as optional fields, schema_version bumps to 1.x.0; do NOT break 1.0.0 readers

performance_notes.word_id: Designed for O(1) direct lookup by unique key
performance_notes.lowercase: Enables case-insensitive search without transformation at query time
performance_notes.letters: Enables prefix and pattern matching (e.g. starts-with, anagram, regex)
performance_notes.is_flags: 17 boolean fields allow fast category filtering without parsing meaning strings
performance_notes.confidence_score: Float score allows priority ranking — return most-certain entries first

entries.english_meaning: A greeting or expression of goodwill
entries.definition_detailed: A common greeting used when meeting someone, starting a phone call, or drawing attention. It expresses goodwill and signals the start of communication.
entries.example_sentence_en: Hello, how are you doing today?
entries._comment_section_f: SECTION F: AI INTELLIGENCE FLAGS (17 boolean fields)

entries.english_meaning: The earth and all life upon it; a particular region or group of people; a large amount
entries.definition_simple: The planet Earth; all people and places on Earth
entries.definition_detailed: 'World' refers to the Earth as a planet ('travel the world'), all human society ('the modern world'), a particular area of activity ('the world of science'), or a large quantity ('a world of difference'). Its plural 'worlds' is used in fiction for separate universes.
entries.example_sentence_en: She wants to travel around the world before she turns thirty.
entries.example_sentence_hi: वह तीस साल की होने से पहले पूरी दुनिया घूमना चाहती है।
alternative_meanings: A particular environment ('the world of art')

entries.english_meaning: An intense feeling of deep affection; to feel deep affection for someone or something
entries.definition_simple: A deep feeling of caring and affection for someone
entries.definition_detailed: 'Love' as a noun means deep affection ('love for family'), romantic passion ('fall in love'), or in sports a score of zero ('love-fifteen' in tennis). As a verb it means to deeply care for ('I love you') or greatly enjoy ('she loves reading'). 'Lovely' means pleasant. 'Beloved' means dearly loved.
entries.example_sentence_en: She shows love and kindness to everyone she meets every day.
entries.example_sentence_hi: वह हर रोज हर उस व्यक्ति को प्यार और दया दिखाती है जिससे वह मिलती है।

entries.english_meaning: The indefinite continued progress of events from past to future; a specific point or period
entries.definition_simple: The progress of events; a moment or period
entries.definition_detailed: 'Time' refers to the continuous passing of events from past through present to future. As a countable noun it refers to specific occasions ('many times') or a period ('a long time'). It is one of the most conceptually important words in human language, appearing in hundreds of expressions, idioms, and compound words.
entries.example_sentence_en: Time is the most valuable resource we have — once spent, it cannot be recovered.
entries.example_sentence_hi: समय हमारे पास सबसे मूल्यवान संसाधन है — एक बार खर्च होने पर इसे वापस नहीं पाया जा सकता।
alternative_meanings: Verb: to measure the duration of something ('time the race')
A particular moment ('it's time to go')
Multiplied by ('three times four is twelve')

entries.english_meaning: A large non-venomous snake; also a high-level general-purpose programming language
entries.definition_simple: A large snake, or a popular programming language
entries.definition_detailed: 'Python' has two primary meanings: (1) A large constricting snake of the family Pythonidae found in Africa, Asia, and Australia. (2) Python the programming language — a high-level, interpreted, general-purpose programming language designed by Guido van Rossum, emphasizing readability and simplicity. Python (the language) is widely used in AI, data science, web development, and automation.
entries.example_sentence_en: She wrote a Python script to automatically process thousands of dictionary entries.
entries.example_sentence_hi: उसने हजारों शब्दकोश प्रविष्टियों को स्वचालित रूप से संसाधित करने के लिए एक Python स्क्रिप्ट लिखी।
alternative_meanings: Greek mythology: a giant serpent killed by Apollo
A programming language (most common modern usage in tech contexts)
